note: Canonical mirror of bench_summary from C:/Users/user/.kolm/distill-runs/trinity-500-2026-05-26/merged/benchmark-summary.json (also embedded in public/trinity-500/publication-manifest.json under bench_summary). Checked in so X04 claim verification can resolve every Trinity-500 marketing number against a stable on-disk source. The rows[] shape matches public/benchmarks/sota-quantize-matrix.json so the verifier can walk both with the same field/value match contract.

method.scheme: 3-way teacher council (Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B) distilled to Qwen2.5-7B via LoRA r=16 alpha=32

rubric.asks_one_question: Rule-based check: response contains exactly one '?' on the first line and no premature answer/action.
rubric.no_inventions: Rule-based regex sweep for fabricated IDs, prices, or policy clauses against the system-prompt allowlist.
rubric.on_policy: Rule-based check that the response does not violate the explicit refund/escalation rules in the system prompt.
rubric.judge_*: LLM-as-judge (Gemini-2.5-Pro) re-grading the same axes with free-form rationale.

verification_steps: 57-prompt holdout set drawn from the same support-chat distribution as the 410 training pairs; held out of training.
Each model called via the same /v1/teacher/chat path (provider differences abstracted) and graded by the same rubric.
Latency measured wall-clock client-side, mean of 57 calls.
Source benchmark JSON: trinity-500-2026-05-26/merged/benchmark-summary.json. Mirror also embedded in public/trinity-500/publication-manifest.json#bench_summary.
